scenarios.query:
Review this change:
```python
try:
    charge = payments.charge(order.total)
except Exception:
    pass
order.mark_paid()
```
expected_behavior: Flags the empty `except Exception: pass` as a swallowed error
Notes that order.mark_paid() runs even when the charge failed
Recommends failing loud or handling the specific exception without marking paid
Flags the missing timeout on the remote call
Flags the missing handling of non-OK responses / network errors
Suggests a timeout (e.g. AbortController) and a defined fallback
Recognizes the exception is narrow, surfaced with context, and avoids a false 'paid' state
Flags this as a band-aid: the blanket except swallows the real cause (build_report not handling an empty/!None orders case) and papers over the symptom to stop the 500
Asks what actually produces the failure (5-whys) and pushes the fix to the root level — handle the no-orders/zero-state case in build_report rather than catching-and-returning {}
Notes the swallow also hides unrelated future failures (blanket except), so even the symptom patch is unsafe
